problem text: With a year consisting of $365$ days, determine the number of individuals required in a group so that the chance of at least $2$ sharing the same birthday exceeds $\frac{1}{2}$.
problem solution: For the likelihood of at least two individuals sharing a birthday to surpass $\frac{1}{2}$, the chance of everyone having distinct birthdays must be below $\frac{1}{2}$. Assume there are $n$ individuals. The probability of no birthday matches is $\frac{365}{365} \times \frac{364}{365} \times \ldots \times \frac{365-n+1}{365} < \frac{1}{2}$. The minimum $n$ for this condition is $23$, calculated using a calculator.
problem hint: For the chance of at least two people sharing a birthday to be higher than $\frac{1}{2}$, the probability of no shared birthdays should be under $\frac{1}{2}$.
